description: This figure presents microscopic digital image correlation (μ-DIC) maps showing the local shear-strain distribution near the phase boundary between δ-ferrite and ultrafine-grained (UFG) γ+α aggregates in the rolling-direction (RD) sample of a low-density medium manganese steel (Fe-7.95Mn-0.25C-4.94Al wt.%). Panels (a) and (b) correspond to two representative regions, each containing four sub-images captured at engineering tensile strains of 5%, 10%, 15%, and 20%. The color scale indicates shear strain from 0% to 30%. Dashed black lines mark the δ-ferrite/UFG γ+α phase boundaries, and red arrows highlight localized strain concentration sites. At 5% strain both phases exhibit low shear strain (blue), whereas at 15% and 20% pronounced strain localization (yellow to red) develops along the phase boundaries and within the UFG aggregate region. The scale bar represents 4 μm. These μ-DIC data support the caption and text statements that the δ-ferrite and UFG γ+α aggregates in the RD sample deform under an iso-strain-like condition, yet strain incompatibility at the phase boundaries generates significant strain concentrations. This localized strain promotes geometrically necessary dislocation accumulation and increases the local driving force for strain-induced martensite nucleation, contributing to the higher martensite transformation kinetics observed in the RD sample.
